note: Crafts of The Given Land/The Pattern Reach (concrete_abstract axis). THINGCRAFT is the concrete-pole craft of the Masons of the Given — only what can be touched is real; the world's great builders and its great skeptics. FORMCRAFT is the abstract-pole craft of the Figurists — pattern, symbol and idea, who hold matter to be the coarsest layer of the real. This is the axis the GM's own narrative REGISTER rides on (SNG-048): concrete places speak plainly, abstract places earn their poetry. Authored by Aevi, SNG-054.

abilities.name: Thingcraft
abilities.narrationHints: He puts his hand on the stone and the stone tells him the truth, because stone does not do anything else.
abilities.description: The concrete-pole mastery: the real is what you can touch, weigh, and set. The world's finest building and its most stubborn skepticism, in one craft.
abilities.notFor: Symbol, glamour, and argument (Formcraft's country); anything that requires believing in what cannot be weighed.

tree.name: The Weighed Thing
tree.grants: Know a thing's true weight, material, soundness and worth by touch — no lie of appearance survives your hand.
tree.cannot: It tells you what a thing IS, never what it means or what it is for.

tree.name: The Set Stone
tree.grants: Set a thing so it STAYS — a wall, a joint, a boundary, a claim. What you set does not shift, sag, or come apart.
tree.cannot: What is set is hard to un-set, including by you, including when you were wrong to set it.

tree.name: The Denial
tree.grants: Deny an abstraction purchase on the real: illusion, glamour, symbol-working and figure all simply fail in your presence. The thing is what it is.
tree.cannot: It denies the abstract INDISCRIMINATELY — including meanings you needed, including a Figurist ally's working, including hope.

abilities.name: Stone-Read
abilities.narrationHints: The material gives up its history under his palm.
abilities.description: Read a material's truth by touch — the first gift of the Given.
abilities.notFor: Reading meaning or intent; anything not physically present.

tree.name: Stone-Read
tree.grants: Know what a material truly is, and whether it is sound.
tree.cannot: Materials, not makers. It says the beam will fail; not who cut it badly.

tree.name: The Long Grain
tree.grants: Read a thing's history in its material — the stresses it took, the repairs, the day it nearly broke.
tree.cannot: History, not future. It reports what happened, not what happens next.

tree.name: The Given Word
tree.grants: The material of a whole structure is legible at once — every fault, every honest joint, every lie the builder told.
tree.cannot: Buildings you love turn out to be full of lies. The Masons say this is why they are hard to live with.

abilities.name: The Plain Fact
abilities.narrationHints: He says the flat thing and the beautiful argument in the room dies of it.
abilities.description: Reduce a soaring abstraction to the plain touchable fact under it — and everyone present sees the fact.
abilities.notFor: Anything that is genuinely NOT reducible to a touchable fact. Some things aren't, and the Masons' great blindness is not believing that.

tree.name: The Plain Fact
tree.grants: Cut through a rhetorical or symbolic working to the concrete fact beneath, out loud, and make it land.
tree.cannot: Some abstractions are TRUE. Reducing them does not unmake them; it only makes you look small when it fails.

tree.name: The Bedrock
tree.grants: Reduce an argument, a glamour, or a doctrine to its physical basis — and if it has none, that becomes visible to all.
tree.cannot: A doctrine with no physical basis is not thereby false. The Masons lose this argument to the Figurists roughly every generation.

tree.name: The Only Real
tree.grants: In your presence the touchable simply IS the real, and abstraction cannot get purchase — no symbol acts, no figure walks.
tree.cannot: You have made a world where nothing means anything. Some people find that a relief, and the ones who do worry the Masons most.

abilities.name: Formcraft
abilities.narrationHints: She draws a shape that is not a picture of anything, and the room rearranges itself to agree with it.
abilities.description: The abstract-pole mastery: pattern, symbol and idea as the FINER layer of the real. To a Figurist, matter is the coarse residue of form.
abilities.notFor: Anything that requires the touchable to be primary; the Masons' Denial defeats it outright.

tree.name: The Pattern Eye
tree.grants: See the pattern, symbol, or structure a thing is an instance OF — the form behind the fact.
tree.cannot: Seeing a form does not give you power over its instance. Not yet.

tree.name: The Figure
tree.grants: Make a symbol that ACTS — a mark, a shape, a figure that does in the world what it means.
tree.cannot: A figure does what it MEANS, not what you wanted it to mean. Be precise, or be surprised.

tree.name: The Argument Made Real
tree.grants: Build an idea into the world so completely that it becomes a fact others must move around — a rule, a boundary, a truth-in-effect.
tree.cannot: The Masons can deny it. Reality can, eventually, decline. And a made truth that was never true rots from the inside.

abilities.name: Pattern-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: The repetition surfaces — this has a shape, and the shape has a name.
abilities.description: See the pattern beneath particulars — the first gift of the Pattern Reach.
abilities.notFor: Reading material (Stone-Read); reading order-as-structure (Order-Sense). This reads FORM.

tree.name: Pattern-Sense
tree.grants: See that a set of particulars is an instance of a pattern, and roughly what pattern.
tree.cannot: Seeing the pattern is not seeing whether it is a true one. Figurists find patterns in noise, constantly.

tree.name: The Named Form
tree.grants: Name the form exactly — and once named, its other instances become visible to you everywhere.
tree.cannot: Once you can see it everywhere, you will see it where it is not.

tree.name: The Formal Reach
tree.grants: Read the form of an event, a life, or a history — the shape it is completing.
tree.cannot: Knowing the shape a life is completing does not permit you to change it, and it is a terrible thing to carry.

abilities.name: The Walking Figure
abilities.narrationHints: The mark she drew stands up off the wall and goes to do the thing it means.
abilities.description: Send a made figure to act at a distance — a symbol given errand and enough form to run it.
abilities.notFor: Making a figure that means what you did not mean; the figure will do the second thing, not the first.

tree.name: The Walking Figure
tree.grants: Send a figure to carry a meaning somewhere — a warning, a ward, a binding — and it arrives and acts.
tree.cannot: It acts once and it is spent. And it acts on what it MEANS.

tree.name: The Standing Figure
tree.grants: The figure persists — it keeps meaning, keeps acting, keeps the ward or the rule in force after you have gone.
tree.cannot: A standing figure is a rule nobody is supervising. It will apply itself to cases you never imagined.

tree.name: The Figure That Learns
tree.grants: The figure adapts its meaning to circumstance — it keeps doing what you MEANT even where you did not foresee.
tree.cannot: Now it is deciding what you meant. The Figurists' oldest disasters all begin with a figure that was quite sure it understood.
